fields.brief_block: “The grant or denial of a motion for new trial is a matter within the sound discretion of the trial court and will not be disturbed if there is any evidence to authorize it.” Hargrave v. State, 311 Ga. App. 852, 855 (2011) (quoting Taylor v. State, 259 Ga. App. 457, 460 (2003)).
fields.finding_analysis: Hargrave v. State directly supports this proposition. In Division 2, addressing the denial of Hargrave's motion for new trial, the court states verbatim: “The grant or denial of a motion for new trial is a matter within the sound discretion of the trial court and will not be disturbed if there is any evidence to authorize it.” The brief's quotation is verbatim, and its attribution is accurate — the opinion itself attributes the standard to Taylor v. State, 259 Ga. App. 457, 460 (2003), exactly as the brief's parenthetical indicates.
